Title: Functional evolution and diversification of CYP82D subfamily members have shaped flavonoid diversification in the genus Scutellaria

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key diversification enzyme; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 6-hydroxylation and 8-hydroxylation of flavonoid substrates, especially naringenin-derived flavanones, and uniquely catalyzes 7-O-demethylation of flavone and flavanone substrates such as tectochrysin, genkwanin, pinostrobin, and sakuranetin.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carthamidin; isocarthamidin; chrysin; apigenin; naringenin; dihydrobaicalein; 7-O-methylbaicalein; 6/8-hydroxylated flavanone products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and immediate precursors that are subsequently glucuronidated/storage forms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Carthamidin; isocarthamidin; dihydrobaicalein; baicalein; norwogonin and related 7-O-glucuronides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavonoids contributing to flavonoid diversity in Scutellaria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 6/8-hydroxylated and 7-O-demethylated flavonoids; redirects metabolic flux toward baicalein/norwogonin-related products and carthamidin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCbaiCYP82D511 catalyzes both 6- and 8-hydroxylation of naringenin, with 6-hydroxylation predominant. 2. It is the only tested CYP82D that catalyzes 7-O-demethylation of flavone and flavanone substrates. 3. In Nicotiana benthamiana, transient expression produced carthamidin from naringenin and chrysin from tectochrysin, confirming in planta activity. 4. It also contributes to dihydrobaicalein formation from pinostrobin through demethylation-linked reactions. 5. It represents a neofunctionalized CYP82D driving flavonoid diversification in Scutellaria.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; ancestral sequence reconstruction/functional comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves in comparative transcript analyses; also implicated in roots where corresponding metabolites accumulate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves from pEAQ-HT
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and leaves, depending on metabolite; dihydrobaicalein notably abundant in S. baicalensis roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with naringenin, pinocembrin, or tectochrysin in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics: National Omics Data Encyclopedia OEP00004992; Transcriptomics: Genome Sequence Archive CRA017681; CRA017652
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme for synthetic biology and metabolic engineering of carthamidin derivatives and 7-O-demethylated flavonoids; useful for pathway redesign to redirect flux toward medicinal flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In Nicotiana benthamiana, endogenous glycosyltransferases convert naringenin to 7-O-naringenin glucoside, competing with target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First reported cytochrome P450-catalyzed 7-O-demethylation of flavonoid substrates. Mutational analysis identified D113, G388, and especially residue 389 as important for flavanone binding and catalysis.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key diversification enzyme; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 6- and 8-hydroxylation of naringenin, with 6-hydroxylation predominant; also developed a C-3' hydroxylation function in flavone assays.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Naringenin; flavone substrates such as chrysin/apigenin contextually tested
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products or immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Carthamidin; isocarthamidin and related hydroxylated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to chemical and medicinal flavonoid diversity in Scutellaria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 4'-hydroxyflavanone-derived 6/8-hydroxylated products in S. barbata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCbarCYP82D511a catalyzes both 6- and 8-hydroxylation of naringenin. 2. It belongs to the neofunctionalized CYP82D511 clade associated with flavanone diversification. 3. It additionally showed development of a C-3' hydroxylation function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, consistent with abundance of 4'-hydroxyflavanones in S. barbata
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme candidate for engineering 6/8-hydroxylated flavanones
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as an ortholog of SCbaiCYP82D511 with divergent catalytic profile after duplication/neofunctionalization.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes flavonoid hydroxylation; shows efficient 6/8-hydroxylation in flavone assays and can convert naringenin mainly to a detectable 6-hydroxylated product with relatively low efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Naringenin; flavone substrates including chrysin/apigenin contextually tested
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target product or immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of 6/8-hydroxylated flavonoids, with limited activity toward naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCbaiCYP82D506 is one of the CYP82Ds with evolved F6H/F8H-related activity. 2. It accepts naringenin but only the 6-hydroxylated product was detectable because of lower catalytic efficiency. 3. It contributes to CYP82D catalytic diversification in S. baicalensis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate auxiliary hydroxylase for combinatorial flavonoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work and this study place it among CYP82D clades with hydroxylation functions.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 8-hydroxylation of flavone substrates and uniquely within non-511 clades uses both chrysin and apigenin; also accepts naringenin and mainly yields a detectable 6-hydroxylated product with low efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target product or immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to structural diversity of medicinal flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expands hydroxylation capacity and substrate range in S. baicalensis flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCbaiCYP82D504c efficiently catalyzes 8-hydroxylation in flavone assays. 2. It is the only non-CYP82D511 tested enzyme using both chrysin and apigenin. 3. It also accepts naringenin, but with lower catalytic efficiency yielding only detectable 6-hydroxylated product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering expanded 8-hydroxylation capacity and substrate breadth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as having more efficient 8-hydroxylation than some paralogs.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Displays multiple CYP82D511 catalytic functions, including all catalytic functions except type IV.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Multiple flavonoid substrates used in clade function assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated and demethylated flavonoid products corresponding to type I-III activities
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target products and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports broad flavonoid diversification in S. mairei but lacks type IV-linked dihydrobaicalein-associated activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCmaiCYP82D511 displayed all catalytic functions except type IV. 2. Its activity pattern was consistent with absence of dihydrobaicalein in S. mairei samples. 3. It supports cross-species functional divergence of CYP82D511.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in S. mairei leaves according to comparative transcript analyses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and roots; leaves accumulate baicalin and baicalein in this species
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative enzyme for reconstructing multi-function CYP82D evolution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Absence of dihydrobaicalein in tissues aligns with functional assay results.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Exclusively exhibits type I catalytic function among tested CYP82D511 orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Multiple flavonoid substrates used in clade function assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target products or immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 6/8-hydroxylated flavonoids linked to type I activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers restricted CYP82D511 catalytic profile consistent with species metabolic specialization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCtapCYP82D511 exhibited only type I catalytic function. 2. This restricted activity supports functional divergence of CYP82D511 orthologs across Scutellaria species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots rich in flavanones in S. tapintzensis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful minimal-function CYP82D511 for dissecting catalytic determinants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as comparative ortholog to define type I-only functionality.
